name: Jira
description: Issue and project tracking platform for software teams, providing issues, projects, workflows, boards, and related services.

auth_methods.note: Jira Cloud supports OAuth 2.0 (3LO), OAuth 2.0 client credentials, API tokens (used with basic auth as email:APItoken), and references legacy OAuth 1.0a; documentation page lists these authentication methods.

credential_access.note: The Atlassian Developer Console allows developers to create apps and obtain OAuth client credentials; API tokens can be created from an Atlassian account (id.atlassian.com) without manual approval. Public docs and console show self-service credential creation; product pricing does not state API access requires paid plan.

api_surface.note: Jira Cloud provides a comprehensive REST API covering issues, projects, users, worklogs, boards, sprints, etc., per the platform root docs. (GraphQL is not documented as public for Jira Cloud REST API.)

buildability.note: Developer docs plus self-serve console and API tokens enable building integrations immediately. Rate limits and plan-specific quotas exist and should be respected but do not block initial development. Marketplace publishing or commercial distribution may require additional vendor steps.
